tracker.Rank: SORT ORDER: ACs ranked by Survey Completion % — highest at top, lowest at bottom.
OUTPUT HAS RECOVERED STRONGLY: 13,825 (14-Jul) → 15,725 (15-Jul) → 15,421 (16-Jul). Back above the 11-Jul level for two days running.
Effort is 5.4 per enumerator per day (27% of the 20/day benchmark) — the best two-day stretch since the 08-Jul peak.
ENUMERATOR DEPLOYMENT HAS FINALLY RESTARTED: 2,782 (14-Jul) → 2,793 (15-Jul) → 2,841 (16-Jul), +59 in two days. Idle pool down 152 → 93.
Ldh-North +36 (152→188), Ldh-West +7, Ldh-South +6, Samrala +21 fully deployed. These are exactly the ACs that were most behind.
BOTTOM 5 BY EFFORT (16-Jul, excluding ACs already near completion):
Ldh-West (5.2) · Ldh-North (5.5) · Gill (4.3) · Samrala (6.1) · Ldh-South (5.7). Gill is now the weakest large AC on effort.
NOTE: 'Days to Clear @ Current Pace' = Pending ÷ surveys done on 16-Jul. '@ 20/enum/day' = Pending ÷ (Assigned Enumerators × 20).
NOTE: 12–16 Jul sheets are 24-hour (9 AM–9 AM) snapshots. 06–11 Jul were ~5 PM snapshots.
NOTE: district target was revised again on 16-Jul: 7,09,089 → 7,04,914 (−4,175). Completion % on this sheet uses the latest target.
Booth columns are from the AC-wise booth listings (see 'Booth Analysis' sheet).

daily_surveys.Rank: RECOVERY CONFIRMED: output is back above the 11-Jul level for two days running.
13,825 (14-Jul) → 15,725 (15-Jul) → 15,421 (16-Jul). This is the strongest two-day stretch since the 08-Jul peak of 17,575.
The three-day slide of 12–14 Jul has been fully reversed. Effort per enumerator is back to 5.4–5.6.
ENUMERATOR STRENGTH IS RISING AGAIN AFTER A THREE-DAY FREEZE: 2,782 → 2,782 → 2,782 → 2,793 → 2,841.
+59 enumerators deployed in the last two days; the idle pool has fallen from 152 to 93. Samrala is now fully deployed.
BEST DAY: 08 July — 17,575.  WORST: 09 July — 4,900 (part-day snapshot).
06-Jul completion % is on the old target, 16-Jul on the latest, so a small part of the headline gain is denominator, not work.
SNAPSHOT-TIME CAVEAT: 06–11 Jul are ~5 PM snapshots; 12–16 Jul are 24-hour (9 AM–9 AM).

progress.Rank: DISTRICT ADDED 31,146 SURVEYS IN 2 DAYS (3,89,360 → 4,20,506) = 5.5 per enumerator per day — the best 2-day window in the run.
Completion moved 55.2% → 59.7% (+4.5 pp). A small part reflects the 16-Jul target cut (−4,175); the rest is real work.
THE PICTURE HAS TURNED ACROSS THE BOARD. Only Gill is drifting — its daily output eased (1,296 → 1,290) and at 4.3/enumerator it is
now the weakest large AC on effort, with 19,602 still pending. Worth a direct word despite its 73% headline.
SAMRALA HAS STABILISED AND REVERSED: the four-day slide (…874 → 808) turned to 1,026 → 1,340 once its 21 idle enumerators were deployed
and the 8th–19th supervisor question was taken up. Clear evidence that the deployment lever works.
LDH-NORTH is the standout mover: +36 enumerators, output 716 → 1,002 → 1,034, and it finally crossed into the high-teens (18.5%).
REMAINING GAP: 93 mapped enumerators still idle (Ldh-North 13, Ldh-West 25, Ldh-Central 33, Ldh-East 7, Gill 6, Sahnewal 4).
Down from 152, but Ldh-Central still has a third of its force unassigned.

deployment_gaps.Remarks: 1 supervisor post(s) unassigned; 12 mapped enumerators idle
93 mapped enumerators idle; 19 supervisor slots unfilled

deployment_gaps.Rank: ENUMERATORS NOT ASSIGNED — 93 idle out of 2,934 mapped, DOWN from 152 on 11–14 July. Deployment has restarted:
Ldh-Central 33 idle (still only 81% assigned)  |  Ldh-West 25 idle (87%)  |  Ldh-North 13 idle (94%)  |  Ldh-East 7  |  Gill 6  |  Sahnewal 4.
Samrala, Ldh-South and Dakha are now fully deployed. Ldh-Central remains the single biggest untapped pool.
AT 20 SURVEYS/DAY, the 93 idle enumerators still represent ~1,860 lost surveys per day — down from 3,040, but not yet zero.
SUPERVISORS — 19 slots vacant (Khanna 12 of 20, Samrala 8 of 19). Khanna is nonetheless at 82.9% complete, so its supervisory
gap is not currently biting; Samrala's has just been partly addressed alongside the enumerator surge.

booth_analysis.Remarks: Over 30% of booths have NOT started; 33 booths have no surveyor mapped
Over 30% of booths have NOT started; 21 booths have no surveyor mapped
Over 30% of booths have NOT started; 52 booths have no surveyor mapped
405 booths not started; 142 booths have no surveyor mapped

Ldh-North 106 of 201 (52.7%)  |  Ldh-Central 91 of 178 (51.1%)  |  Ldh-West 68 of 192 (35.4%)  |  Ldh-South 45 of 174 (25.9%)
Sahnewal 35  |  Samrala 26  |  Ldh-East 22  |  Gill 5  |  Jagraon 4  |  Atam Nagar 3
ZERO not-started booths: Dakha, Raikot, Khanna, Payal.
BOOTHS WITH NO SURVEYOR MAPPED — 142 booths. These booths CANNOT start at all:
Ldh-Central 52  |  Ldh-West 33  |  Samrala 23  |  Ldh-North 21  |  Ldh-East 6  |  Sahnewal 4  |  Ldh-South 3
This mirrors the 152-enumerator deployment gap. Fixing it is the single highest-return action available.
BOOTHS AT 100% — 518 of 2,927 (17.7%). Payal 156 of 207 (75.4%) and Dakha 101 of 217 (46.5%) are closing out.
NOTE: booth listings are a snapshot around 11–12 July, so booth survey totals lag the 16-Jul daily sheet by several days.
Booth COUNTS are the reliable figure here; survey totals should be read from the Tracker sheet.
